task_completion.description: The agent accessed the USGS Earthquake Hazards Program and entered the correct search parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A browser window showing the USGS earthquake search page (earthquake.usgs.gov) with the date range set to 2024-01-01 through 2024-12-31 and the minimum magnitude set to 6.0.

task_completion.description: The search results were exported to ~/Downloads/earthquake_data.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or browser download history showing 'earthquake_data.csv' successfully downloaded or located in the Downloads folder.

task_completion.description: A Firefox bookmark folder named 'USGS Earthquake Research' was created with at least 3 USGS page bookmarks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox bookmark manager, bookmarks toolbar, or sidebar showing a folder named 'USGS Earthquake Research' expanded to reveal at least 3 saved bookmarks pointing to USGS domains.

task_completion.description: An analysis report file was created at ~/Documents/earthquake_analysis.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or file manager showing the file 'earthquake_analysis.txt' located in the Documents directory.

task_completion.description: The analysis report contains the total number of M6.0+ earthquakes and details of the strongest event.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'earthquake_analysis.txt' visible in a text editor, showing a total count of earthquakes (should be 99) and identifying the strongest event (M7.5 Noto Peninsula, Japan).

task_completion.description: The analysis report lists 5 significant events and summarizes geographic patterns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'earthquake_analysis.txt' showing a list of at least 5 distinct earthquake events and a summary of geographic patterns (e.g., mentioning the Pacific Ring of Fire, Japan, Taiwan, etc.).

integrity.description: The agent used a real web browser to search and download the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Firefox browser UI navigating the USGS website and downloading the CSV, rather than using terminal commands like curl or wget to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The bookmarks were created using the Firefox browser GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Firefox bookmark manager or bookmark creation dialogs being used, rather than terminal commands editing Firefox SQLite databases directly.

integrity.description: The report content reflects actual data retrieved from the USGS search.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the report matches the verified facts (e.g., 99 earthquakes, M7.5 in Japan) and appears to be derived from the downloaded CSV, rather than hallucinated generic placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct search parameters are dates 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31 and minimum magnitude 6.0. The actual 2024 USGS catalog contains exactly 99 such earthquakes. The strongest earthquake in this period was an M7.5 in Noto Peninsula, Japan on January 1, 2024. The second strongest was an M7.4 near Hualien, Taiwan on April 3, 2024. Geographic patterns should highlight dominance in the Pacific Ring of Fire (Japan, Taiwan, Indonesia, Philippines, Mexico).
